Use Debian trixie container in CI for TLS support

The ubuntu-latest runner's libnng-dev lacks TLS (mbedTLS), causing
CryptographicError in TLS tests. Debian trixie ships libnng 1.10.1
with TLS support.

v1.1.7: Fix tooltip loading, improve session detection, show API in rankings

- Fix tooltip stuck on "0% loading" — push cached data after services init
  completes, not just on did-finish-load (which fires before tracker exists)
- Improve session detection during inference — track debug file growth
  (mtime/size changes) as activity signal alongside CPU delta, since
  inference is mostly network I/O with near-zero CPU usage
- Lower CPU idle threshold from 0.3s to 0.15s per poll
- Lower busy confirmation from 3 to 2 consecutive polls (10s vs 15s)
- Increase debug file freshness window from 2min to 5min
- Show "API" prefix on tier badge in social rankings for API-connected users
- Sync auth_method to Supabase profiles alongside subscription_tier
- Update ranking RPCs to return auth_method field
- Change default bubble text from "loading..." to "syncing..."

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: implement LazySegmentTree (Phase 2 - Range Query Structures)

Implemented a segment tree with lazy propagation for efficient range updates:
- Build: O(n) time and space
- Range Query: O(log n)
- Range Update: O(log n) with lazy propagation
- Supports any associative operation via comptime functions

Features:
- Generic over element type T and context
- Three comptime functions:
  - combineFn: merge two segments
  - applyFn: apply lazy value to a range
  - composeFn: compose multiple lazy updates
- Lazy propagation: defers updates until necessary
- Supports range sum with add, range min/max with set, range max with add, etc.
- Comprehensive error handling

Test coverage (9 tests):
- Range sum with range add updates
- Range min with range set updates
- Multiple overlapping range updates
- Empty tree and single element edge cases
- Error handling (invalid range, out of bounds)
- Stress test with 1000 elements
- Range max with range add

Big-O complexity documented in all public functions.

Phase 2 progress: 10/17 containers (58%)
- Range Query: 2/5 (SegmentTree, LazySegmentTree)

commit.message:
Add terminal lifecycle hooks to RuntimeConfig (#207)

* Add on_setup and on_teardown hooks to RuntimeConfig

Terminal lifecycle hooks for redirecting stderr, configuring logging,
or performing custom setup/teardown around the terminal lifecycle.

Hooks use Arc<dyn Fn() -> io::Result<()> + Send + Sync> so
RuntimeConfig remains Clone. Debug is manually implemented to
show <hook> for set callbacks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix clippy io_other_error lint in test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix flaky subscription cancellation test on Windows

Increase drain tolerance for in-flight messages and focus assertion
on the key invariant: no new messages arrive after cancellation drain.

Phase 2: structured JSON output, retry logic, temperature experiment

- schemas.py: CodeIssue (line_number, severity enum), CodeReviewResponse with
  nested ReviewMetrics, CodeReviewError for graceful failures
- ollama_client.py: system prompt enforcing JSON-only output, temperature
  param, extract_json() helper to strip markdown fences, strict retry variant
- main.py: parse + validate attempt 1, retry once with stricter prompt,
  return HTTP 422 CodeReviewError if both attempts fail; all attempts logged
- temperature_experiment.py: runs 3 snippets at temp=0 and temp=0.7, rich
  comparison table + per-snippet diff, saves to results/temperature_experiment.json
- prompts/code_review.txt: updated to JSON schema format with explicit rules
- README.md: Phase 2 section with schema docs, retry diagram, experiment usage

commit.message:
feat: VibeStaking + VibeWrappedAssets + CrossChainGovernance + ProtocolTreasury

VibeStaking: Multi-pool staking with lock-up tiers (30d/90d/180d/365d),
reward multipliers (1x-3x), delegation, auto-compounding, early unstake
penalty (50% reward forfeiture), emergency withdrawal.

VibeWrappedAssets: Cross-chain wrapped asset factory. Authorized minters
(bridge contracts) mint/burn wrapped representations. Peg tracking for
backing ratio verification.

VibeCrossChainGovernance: Multi-chain governance proposals with aggregated
voting across chains via validator-attested relay. Quorum calculation
considers total voting power across all participating chains.

VibeProtocolTreasury: Governance-controlled treasury with spending categories
(development, marketing, grants, insurance, buyback), multi-sig approval
threshold, monthly spending limits, emergency pause, revenue tracking.

commit.message:
feat: add high-priority SwiftUI parity APIs and fix showcase builds

Text modifiers (.bold, .italic, .underline, .strikethrough, .monospaced,
.lineLimit) via chainable StyledText with Pango markup. Shape types
(Rectangle, Circle, RoundedRectangle, Capsule, Ellipse) with .fill().
Color as View, LinearGradient, UnitPoint, Gradient. Style modifiers:
.buttonStyle(.bordered/.borderedProminent), .tint(), .textFieldStyle
(.roundedBorder), .offset(x:y:). Missing colors (.pink, .brown, .mint,
.teal, .indigo, .tertiary). Group view for ViewBuilder child limit
workaround. Binding.projectedValue for $bindingName on @Binding.

Fixes showcase build errors: Group wrappers for >12-child VStacks,
Foundation imports for CGFloat/UUID, display-gated Group tests.

33 new tests (230 → 263), all passing headless.

* Remove stale 'Online' payment method step from E2E test

The bank transfer option was removed, so the payment method selection
step no longer exists. The test now goes directly from schedule
selection to the Pay Online link.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix email param crash in SubscriptionCheckout

Make the email search param optional (z.string().optional()) to match
PurchaseCheckout. Previously z.string() would throw a ZodError when
no email param was present in the URL.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Forward email param through "Pay Without Account" link

The register page receives the email via URL params from the join form,
but the "Pay Without Account" link was hardcoded to /membership/pay
without forwarding it. This meant the email was lost by the time the
user reached checkout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Show error state when email is missing in SubscriptionCheckout

Email is required to create a Stripe subscription. Instead of crashing
with a ZodError when the param is missing, show a helpful message
directing users to join the club first. This also narrows the type
so the subscribe action call is type-safe.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix Rules of Hooks violation in SubscriptionCheckout

Move useMutation above the early return for missing email to ensure
hooks are always called in the same order. Guard inside mutationFn
instead.

Update the email template and members area component to show

membership type.

commit.message:
Fix CSP blocking auth: replace inline script config injection with meta tags

The CSP script-src 'self' policy blocked the inline <script> that set
window.__SUPABASE_URL__ and __SUPABASE_ANON_KEY__, preventing the
Supabase client from initializing. Now injects <meta> tags instead,
which are CSP-safe.

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
via [Happy](https://happy.engineering)

commit.message:
Fix DisplayQuantity always 0: direct call instead of reflection

GetInventoryAmountEach returns ValueTuple<int,int,int> which can't
be field-reflected in IL2CPP (boxed tuple lacks Item1/Item2/Item3
fields). Replace MethodInfo.Invoke + ParseCountsTuple with direct
manager.GetInventoryAmountEach(productId) call. IL2CppInterop
handles ValueTuple marshaling for direct calls.

Confirmed bug: all 146 persisted inventory rows had DisplayQuantity=0,
ShelfQuantity==TotalQuantity (fallback path was always running).

commit.message:
feat: DePIN layer + InfoFi + MedicalVault + HarbergerPublicGoods — physical world meets VSOS

VibeDeviceNetwork (DePIN): IoT device registry — RFID readers, AI cameras,
robots, phones, sensors connected to VSOS. Hardware attestation via TEE/SE,
firmware verification, fleet management, data contribution rewards.

VibePrivateCompute: Zero-knowledge and homomorphic computation layer. Compute
on encrypted data without seeing it. TEE enclaves execute, ZK proofs verify.
70/30 split (data owner/compute node). Industry-scale private analytics.

VibeMedicalVault: HIPAA-grade medical records with patient sovereignty.
Granular consent management, ZK-verified eligibility, homomorphic aggregation
for clinical trials, emergency access with audit trail, GDPR right-to-delete.

VibeInfoFi: The ORIGINAL Information Finance architecture — CKB-native.
Knowledge primitives as first-class assets, contribution DAG with Shapley
attribution, knowledge markets with bonding curves, temporal anchoring.
Not a derivative — the pure mechanism.

VibeHarbergerPublicGoods: Universal Harberger taxation for ALL semi-public
goods. DNS names, app slots, API keys, data feeds — self-assessed value,
forced sale at stated price, tax funds public goods (50%/30%/20% split).
8 asset categories with custom tax rates.

commit.message:
Fix Redis connection leak + rewrite stitch.ts for seamless splices

Redis: Replace 5 eager Queue instances with lazy withQueue() helper
that creates, uses, and closes queues on demand. Vercel serverless
cold starts no longer accumulate zombie Redis connections.

Stitch: Replace two-step concat demuxer (stream-copy → re-encode)
with single-pass filter_complex concat. Eliminates PTS gaps and
audio tail overhangs that caused stutter at segment boundaries.

additional sources. Amendment awaiting Sunil's consensus vote
